enriched_points.expanded_content: The Budapest Convention, established in 2001, is the first binding international treaty aimed at combating cybercrime, setting a global standard for legal frameworks.
It includes provisions for criminalizing various cyber offenses, procedural measures for investigations, and mutual legal assistance among member states to enhance cooperation.
The Convention fosters international collaboration, enabling member states to effectively address cyber threats, share best practices, and protect electronic evidence across jurisdictions.
